input.plan:
# Plan: Todo #12 — Config discoverability + scalar config hierarchy

## Context

vc-x1 has two independent config systems and no way to discover or
validate either:

- **User config** `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml` → parsed into the
  typed `UserConfig` struct (`src/config.rs`).
- **Workspace config** `<root>/.vc-config.toml` → never parsed into
  a struct; ~10 readers pull individual dotted keys straight out of
  a flat `HashMap<String,String>` (`toml_simple`, all values
  strings).

Problems this cycle fixes:

- **Undiscoverable.** Settable keys (`[workspace]` path/other-repo,
  `[push]` state-dir/state-file, `[bot-session].items`, plus the
  scalars added below) are only documented in prose; unknown keys
  are silently ignored, so typos fail silently.
- **No single source of truth.** Any generated config file with
  live default values would pin choices at generation time and go
  stale on upgrade.
- **Inconsistent resolution.** `[bot-session].items` resolves
  CLI > workspace > user > built-in, but the scalar knobs
  `--result-lines` and `--col-width` (shipped in 0.70.1 / 0.70.3)
  are plain clap defaults with no config layer.

**Keystone idea:** one declared **config schema registry** in code.
The `config` command, init's commented-defaults block, and the
`--validate` check all *derive* from it, so they cannot drift
("derived from the installed binary, cannot drift").

**Decisions locked with the user:**
- One `config` subcommand with modes (`vc-x1 config`,
  `config --home <h>`, `config --validate`), not separate commands.
- Full five-part ladder in one cycle.

**Model delegation (per user):** planning by Fable 5; implementation
dropped to cheaper models. Suggested tier per step below — default
**Sonnet** for the work commits (Haiku only for the most mechanical
edits), Fable 5 retained for each per-commit-flow work/description
review and the close-out narrative.

## Version & cycle shape

New feature ladder, version-of-record **0.71.0** (`Cargo.toml`
`[package].version`). Follows the cycle protocol
(`notes/cycle-protocol.md`): Preparation `0.71.0-0` → Work
`0.71.0-1..5` → Close-out bare `0.71.0`. Each work commit runs the
cargo cycle (fmt → clippy -D warnings → test → install --locked).

## The ladder

### 0.71.0-0 — Preparation *(Sonnet / bookkeeping)*
- Bump `Cargo.toml` 0.70.3 → 0.71.0-0 (and `Cargo.lock`).
- Move Todo #12 from `## Todo` into `## In Progress` (bold title,
  problem statement, the plan ladder below).
- Open the chores section in `notes/chores/chores-13.md`:
  `## feat: config discoverability + scalar hierarchy` (working
  close-out title) with an empty `Commits:` line.
- Backfill the 0.70.3 `--col-width` section's `Commits:` (now
  permanent on `main`) — one-push-later cadence.

### 0.71.0-1 — bot-session scalar config keys *(Sonnet)*
The deferred hierarchy piece; independent of the registry, smallest
self-contained change, done first.
- `src/config.rs`: add `bot_session_result_lines: Option<usize>` and
  `bot_session_col_width: Option<usize>` to `UserConfig`
  (`src/config.rs:78-100`). Parse in `load_from` (~`:159`):
  `map.get("bot-session.result-lines")` / `col-width`, `.parse::<usize>()`,
  **error on a bad value** (consistent with malformed-file = fatal).
  Update the three `UserConfig` struct-literal test sites
  (`~:618`, `~:758`, and any other).
- `src/bot_session.rs`: args `result_lines`/`col_width` become
  `Option<usize>` (drop `default_value_t`; help text notes the
  default + config key). Generalize `workspace_items()`
  (`:766-776`) → `workspace_bot_session()` returning a small struct
  `{ items, result_lines, col_width }` (parse the two usize keys).
  `BotSessionParams` stores the **raw `Option<usize>`** for each;
  resolve in the op `bot_session()` (`:428`, has `ctx`) —
  `cli.or(workspace).or(user).unwrap_or(BUILTIN_CONST)` — mirroring
  how `resolve_items` already layers toggles > workspace > user >
  builtin. Pass the resolved `usize` into `fields_view` /
  `per_line_view` / `render`. Keep `COL_WIDTH` / `RESULT_LINE_CAP`
  as the built-in constants.
- `README.md`: note both scalars now honor the hierarchy.
- Tests (`tests/cli_bot_session.rs`): workspace + user config
  resolution for `col-width` / `result-lines`, CLI-overrides-config.

### 0.71.0-2 — config schema registry *(Sonnet; accuracy-critical)*
The single source of truth. No user-visible behavior yet.
- New module `src/config_schema.rs` (declared in `src/main.rs`
  `mod` block). Shape:
  ```rust
  pub enum Home { User, WorkspaceCode, WorkspaceBot }
  pub enum ValueKind { Str, Usize, ItemList }
  pub struct ConfigKey {
      pub path: &'static str,        // "bot-session.col-width"
      pub homes: &'static [Home],
      pub kind: ValueKind,
      pub default: Option<&'static str>, // rendered default, or None
      pub required: bool,            // active (not commented) in init
      pub dynamic: bool,             // repo.category.<cat> / account.<name>.*
      pub doc: &'static str,         // one line
  }
  pub fn schema() -> &'static [ConfigKey];
  ```
- Enumerate every settable key from the surface map:
  - **User**: `default.account`, `default.debug` (mark reserved),
    `repo.default`, `repo.category.<cat>` (dynamic),
    `account.<name>.repo.*` (dynamic), `bot-session.items`,
    `bot-session.result-lines`, `bot-session.col-width`.
  - **Workspace (code+bot)**: `workspace.path` (required,
    role-specific default `/` vs `/.claude`), `workspace.other-repo`
    (dual only), `push.state-dir` (default `.vc-x1`),
    `push.state-file` (default `push-state.toml`),
    `bot-session.items`, `bot-session.result-lines`,
    `bot-session.col-width`.
- Numeric defaults reference the real consts by an equality **unit
  test** (`"68"` == `COL_WIDTH.to_string()`,
  `DEFAULT_STATE_DIR`/`DEFAULT_STATE_FILE` from `src/push.rs`) so a
  const change that outruns the schema string fails CI.
- Unit tests: unique paths, non-empty homes, defaults-match-consts.

### 0.71.0-3 — `config` command: print annotated schema *(Sonnet)*
- New module `src/config_cmd.rs` (module name `config` is taken by
  the loader). Follow the subcommand template
  (`src/validate_desc.rs`): `#[derive(Args)] ConfigArgs`, flat
  `ConfigParams`, `From<&ConfigArgs>`, free
  `fn config(ctx, params) -> Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>`,
  `impl SubcommandRunner`.
- Wire into `src/main.rs` (three spots: `mod config_cmd;`, enum
  variant `Config(config_cmd::ConfigArgs)`, dispatch arm).
- `--home user|workspace|all` (default `all`). Render the schema
  sshd_config style, grouped by home then TOML section, emitting via
  `info!` (stdout). Show `#key = default  # doc` for optional keys,
  active `key = default` for required, `<cat>`/`<name>` placeholders
  for dynamic keys.
- Tests: `tests/cli_config.rs` (asserts known keys + defaults
  appear; `--home` filters).

### 0.71.0-4 — init emits commented defaults *(Sonnet; init tests finicky)*
- `src/init.rs`: replace the hand-written settable-key portion of
  `VC_CONFIG_CODE` / `VC_CONFIG_SESSION` / `VC_CONFIG_APP_ONLY`
  (`:294-338`) with a generator `render_vc_config(role)` that emits
  the required active `[workspace]` block (as today) **plus** a
  commented block of the optional keys for that role, rendered from
  `config_schema::schema()`. Required keys stay active; optional keys
  are `#key = default` placeholders — documents the surface, pins
  nothing.
- Update the init tests that byte-compare against the old constants
  (and the repo's own `.vc-config.toml` / `.claude/.vc-config.toml`
  fixtures) to the new generated output, or assert-by-parse +
  contains rather than exact-match.

### 0.71.0-5 — `config --validate` unknown-key check *(Sonnet)*
- `src/config_cmd.rs`: add `--validate` mode. Load the resolved
  config file(s) — user `config_path()` and the workspace
  `.vc-config.toml` (via `find_workspace_root`) — with
  `toml_simple::toml_load`, and flag any key not in the schema
  (honoring the `dynamic` patterns for `repo.category.*` /
  `account.*`). Report each unknown key with its file and, if cheap,
  a nearest-known-key suggestion. Exit non-zero when unknown keys
  are found (opt-in strict check; the normal load path keeps
  silently ignoring them for forward-compat).
- Tests: a file with a good key + a typo'd key → reports the typo,
  exits non-zero; a clean file → exits zero.

### 0.71.0 — Close-out *(Fable 5)*
- `README.md`: document the `config` command (print + `--home` +
  `--validate`) and the init commented-defaults behavior.
- `TODO.md`: move Todo #12 → one-line `## Done` entry (chores `[N]`
  ref); replace the In Progress block into the chores section with
  an `### As-built ladder`; promote the schema-registry rationale to
  a `### Single source of truth` design subsection.
- `notes/README.md`: update if the command list changed.

## Critical files
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig`, `load_from` (scalar keys).
- `src/bot_session.rs` — args/params/resolution, `workspace_items` →
  `workspace_bot_session`.
- `src/config_schema.rs` *(new)* — the registry (keystone).
- `src/config_cmd.rs` *(new)* — the `config` command.
- `src/init.rs` — `render_vc_config` generator (`:294-357`).
- `src/main.rs` — subcommand wiring (`mod`, enum, dispatch).
- Tests: `tests/cli_bot_session.rs`, `tests/cli_config.rs` *(new)*,
  `tests/cli_init.rs`.

## Reuse (don't reinvent)
- Subcommand template: `src/validate_desc.rs` (From shape) /
  `src/chid.rs` (TryFrom shape); trait in `src/subcommand.rs:48`.
- Config-home layering: mirror `resolve_items`
  (`src/bot_session.rs:783`) for the scalar resolution.
- Value parsing: `toml_simple::toml_load` / `toml_get`
  (`src/toml_simple.rs`); consumers `.parse()` strings themselves —
  see `src/sync/state.rs:97`.
- Workspace root / readers: `find_workspace_root`
  (`src/common.rs:677`), `VC_CONFIG_FILE` (`src/desc_helpers.rs:12`),
  push defaults `DEFAULT_STATE_DIR`/`DEFAULT_STATE_FILE`
  (`src/push.rs:263-266`).
- Output: `info!` → stdout (`src/logging.rs:83`).

## Verification (end-to-end)
- Each work commit: `cargo fmt && cargo clippy --all-targets -D
  warnings && cargo test && cargo install --path . --locked`.
- `-1`: with `[bot-session] col-width = 30` in a temp
  `.vc-config.toml`, `vc-x1 bot-session --per-line FILE` pads to 30;
  a CLI `--col-width 50` overrides it; a bad value errors.
- `-3`: `vc-x1 config` lists every schema key with defaults;
  `vc-x1 config --home user` filters.
- `-4`: `vc-x1 init` a throwaway dir; the generated `.vc-config.toml`
  parses and carries the commented optional keys.
- `-5`: `vc-x1 config --validate` on a file with a typo'd key
  reports it and exits non-zero; clean file exits zero.
- Close-out: `vc-x1 -V` reports `0.71.0`; full `cargo test` green.

## Out of scope (note in close-out, not built)
- Printing the *resolved/effective* merged config (vs. the schema of
  defaults) — a possible follow-up.
- Reworking the untyped workspace map into a typed `WorkspaceConfig`
  struct — the registry gives discoverability without that churn.

# Plan: Todo #12 — Config discoverability + scalar config hierarchy

## Context

vc-x1 has two independent config systems and no way to discover or
validate either:

- **User config** `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml` → parsed into the
  typed `UserConfig` struct (`src/config.rs`).
- **Workspace config** `<root>/.vc-config.toml` → never parsed into
  a struct; ~10 readers pull individual dotted keys straight out of
  a flat `HashMap<String,String>` (`toml_simple`, all values
  strings).

Problems this cycle fixes:

- **Undiscoverable.** Settable keys (`[workspace]` path/other-repo,
  `[push]` state-dir/state-file, `[bot-session].items`, plus the
  scalars added below) are only documented in prose; unknown keys
  are silently ignored, so typos fail silently.
- **No single source of truth.** Any generated config file with
  live default values would pin choices at generation time and go
  stale on upgrade.
- **Inconsistent resolution.** `[bot-session].items` resolves
  CLI > workspace > user > built-in, but the scalar knobs
  `--result-lines` and `--col-width` (shipped in 0.70.1 / 0.70.3)
  are plain clap defaults with no config layer.

**Keystone idea:** one declared **config schema registry** in code.
The `config` command, init's commented-defaults block, and the
`--validate` check all *derive* from it, so they cannot drift
("derived from the installed binary, cannot drift").

**Decisions locked with the user:**
- One `config` subcommand with modes (`vc-x1 config`,
  `config --home <h>`, `config --validate`), not separate commands.
- Full five-part ladder in one cycle.

**Model delegation (per user):** planning by Fable 5; implementation
dropped to cheaper models. Suggested tier per step below — default
**Sonnet** for the work commits (Haiku only for the most mechanical
edits), Fable 5 retained for each per-commit-flow work/description
review and the close-out narrative.

## Version & cycle shape

New feature ladder, version-of-record **0.71.0** (`Cargo.toml`
`[package].version`). Follows the cycle protocol
(`notes/cycle-protocol.md`): Preparation `0.71.0-0` → Work
`0.71.0-1..5` → Close-out bare `0.71.0`. Each work commit runs the
cargo cycle (fmt → clippy -D warnings → test → install --locked).

## The ladder

### 0.71.0-0 — Preparation *(Sonnet / bookkeeping)*
- Bump `Cargo.toml` 0.70.3 → 0.71.0-0 (and `Cargo.lock`).
- Move Todo #12 from `## Todo` into `## In Progress` (bold title,
  problem statement, the plan ladder below).
- Open the chores section in `notes/chores/chores-13.md`:
  `## feat: config discoverability + scalar hierarchy` (working
  close-out title) with an empty `Commits:` line.
- Backfill the 0.70.3 `--col-width` section's `Commits:` (now
  permanent on `main`) — one-push-later cadence.

### 0.71.0-1 — bot-session scalar config keys *(Sonnet)*
The deferred hierarchy piece; independent of the registry, smallest
self-contained change, done first.
- `src/config.rs`: add `bot_session_result_lines: Option<usize>` and
  `bot_session_col_width: Option<usize>` to `UserConfig`
  (`src/config.rs:78-100`). Parse in `load_from` (~`:159`):
  `map.get("bot-session.result-lines")` / `col-width`, `.parse::<usize>()`,
  **error on a bad value** (consistent with malformed-file = fatal).
  Update the three `UserConfig` struct-literal test sites
  (`~:618`, `~:758`, and any other).
- `src/bot_session.rs`: args `result_lines`/`col_width` become
  `Option<usize>` (drop `default_value_t`; help text notes the
  default + config key). Generalize `workspace_items()`
  (`:766-776`) → `workspace_bot_session()` returning a small struct
  `{ items, result_lines, col_width }` (parse the two usize keys).
  `BotSessionParams` stores the **raw `Option<usize>`** for each;
  resolve in the op `bot_session()` (`:428`, has `ctx`) —
  `cli.or(workspace).or(user).unwrap_or(BUILTIN_CONST)` — mirroring
  how `resolve_items` already layers toggles > workspace > user >
  builtin. Pass the resolved `usize` into `fields_view` /
  `per_line_view` / `render`. Keep `COL_WIDTH` / `RESULT_LINE_CAP`
  as the built-in constants.
- `README.md`: note both scalars now honor the hierarchy.
- Tests (`tests/cli_bot_session.rs`): workspace + user config
  resolution for `col-width` / `result-lines`, CLI-overrides-config.

### 0.71.0-2 — config schema registry *(Sonnet; accuracy-critical)*
The single source of truth. No user-visible behavior yet.
- New module `src/config_schema.rs` (declared in `src/main.rs`
  `mod` block). Shape:
  ```rust
  pub enum Home { User, WorkspaceCode, WorkspaceBot }
  pub enum ValueKind { Str, Usize, ItemList }
  pub struct ConfigKey {
      pub path: &'static str,        // "bot-session.col-width"
      pub homes: &'static [Home],
      pub kind: ValueKind,
      pub default: Option<&'static str>, // rendered default, or None
      pub required: bool,            // active (not commented) in init
      pub dynamic: bool,             // repo.category.<cat> / account.<name>.*
      pub doc: &'static str,         // one line
  }
  pub fn schema() -> &'static [ConfigKey];
  ```
- Enumerate every settable key from the surface map:
  - **User**: `default.account`, `default.debug` (mark reserved),
    `repo.default`, `repo.category.<cat>` (dynamic),
    `account.<name>.repo.*` (dynamic), `bot-session.items`,
    `bot-session.result-lines`, `bot-session.col-width`.
  - **Workspace (code+bot)**: `workspace.path` (required,
    role-specific default `/` vs `/.claude`), `workspace.other-repo`
    (dual only), `push.state-dir` (default `.vc-x1`),
    `push.state-file` (default `push-state.toml`),
    `bot-session.items`, `bot-session.result-lines`,
    `bot-session.col-width`.
- Numeric defaults reference the real consts by an equality **unit
  test** (`"68"` == `COL_WIDTH.to_string()`,
  `DEFAULT_STATE_DIR`/`DEFAULT_STATE_FILE` from `src/push.rs`) so a
  const change that outruns the schema string fails CI.
- Unit tests: unique paths, non-empty homes, defaults-match-consts.

### 0.71.0-3 — `config` command: print annotated schema *(Sonnet)*
- New module `src/config_cmd.rs` (module name `config` is taken by
  the loader). Follow the subcommand template
  (`src/validate_desc.rs`): `#[derive(Args)] ConfigArgs`, flat
  `ConfigParams`, `From<&ConfigArgs>`, free
  `fn config(ctx, params) -> Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>`,
  `impl SubcommandRunner`.
- Wire into `src/main.rs` (three spots: `mod config_cmd;`, enum
  variant `Config(config_cmd::ConfigArgs)`, dispatch arm).
- `--home user|workspace|all` (default `all`). Render the schema
  sshd_config style, grouped by home then TOML section, emitting via
  `info!` (stdout). Show `#key = default  # doc` for optional keys,
  active `key = default` for required, `<cat>`/`<name>` placeholders
  for dynamic keys.
- Tests: `tests/cli_config.rs` (asserts known keys + defaults
  appear; `--home` filters).

### 0.71.0-4 — init emits commented defaults *(Sonnet; init tests finicky)*
- `src/init.rs`: replace the hand-written settable-key portion of
  `VC_CONFIG_CODE` / `VC_CONFIG_SESSION` / `VC_CONFIG_APP_ONLY`
  (`:294-338`) with a generator `render_vc_config(role)` that emits
  the required active `[workspace]` block (as today) **plus** a
  commented block of the optional keys for that role, rendered from
  `config_schema::schema()`. Required keys stay active; optional keys
  are `#key = default` placeholders — documents the surface, pins
  nothing.
- Update the init tests that byte-compare against the old constants
  (and the repo's own `.vc-config.toml` / `.claude/.vc-config.toml`
  fixtures) to the new generated output, or assert-by-parse +
  contains rather than exact-match.

### 0.71.0-5 — `config --validate` unknown-key check *(Sonnet)*
- `src/config_cmd.rs`: add `--validate` mode. Load the resolved
  config file(s) — user `config_path()` and the workspace
  `.vc-config.toml` (via `find_workspace_root`) — with
  `toml_simple::toml_load`, and flag any key not in the schema
  (honoring the `dynamic` patterns for `repo.category.*` /
  `account.*`). Report each unknown key with its file and, if cheap,
  a nearest-known-key suggestion. Exit non-zero when unknown keys
  are found (opt-in strict check; the normal load path keeps
  silently ignoring them for forward-compat).
- Tests: a file with a good key + a typo'd key → reports the typo,
  exits non-zero; a clean file → exits zero.

### 0.71.0 — Close-out *(Fable 5)*
- `README.md`: document the `config` command (print + `--home` +
  `--validate`) and the init commented-defaults behavior.
- `TODO.md`: move Todo #12 → one-line `## Done` entry (chores `[N]`
  ref); replace the In Progress block into the chores section with
  an `### As-built ladder`; promote the schema-registry rationale to
  a `### Single source of truth` design subsection.
- `notes/README.md`: update if the command list changed.

## Critical files
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig`, `load_from` (scalar keys).
- `src/bot_session.rs` — args/params/resolution, `workspace_items` →
  `workspace_bot_session`.
- `src/config_schema.rs` *(new)* — the registry (keystone).
- `src/config_cmd.rs` *(new)* — the `config` command.
- `src/init.rs` — `render_vc_config` generator (`:294-357`).
- `src/main.rs` — subcommand wiring (`mod`, enum, dispatch).
- Tests: `tests/cli_bot_session.rs`, `tests/cli_config.rs` *(new)*,
  `tests/cli_init.rs`.

## Reuse (don't reinvent)
- Subcommand template: `src/validate_desc.rs` (From shape) /
  `src/chid.rs` (TryFrom shape); trait in `src/subcommand.rs:48`.
- Config-home layering: mirror `resolve_items`
  (`src/bot_session.rs:783`) for the scalar resolution.
- Value parsing: `toml_simple::toml_load` / `toml_get`
  (`src/toml_simple.rs`); consumers `.parse()` strings themselves —
  see `src/sync/state.rs:97`.
- Workspace root / readers: `find_workspace_root`
  (`src/common.rs:677`), `VC_CONFIG_FILE` (`src/desc_helpers.rs:12`),
  push defaults `DEFAULT_STATE_DIR`/`DEFAULT_STATE_FILE`
  (`src/push.rs:263-266`).
- Output: `info!` → stdout (`src/logging.rs:83`).

## Verification (end-to-end)
- Each work commit: `cargo fmt && cargo clippy --all-targets -D
  warnings && cargo test && cargo install --path . --locked`.
- `-1`: with `[bot-session] col-width = 30` in a temp
  `.vc-config.toml`, `vc-x1 bot-session --per-line FILE` pads to 30;
  a CLI `--col-width 50` overrides it; a bad value errors.
- `-3`: `vc-x1 config` lists every schema key with defaults;
  `vc-x1 config --home user` filters.
- `-4`: `vc-x1 init` a throwaway dir; the generated `.vc-config.toml`
  parses and carries the commented optional keys.
- `-5`: `vc-x1 config --validate` on a file with a typo'd key
  reports it and exits non-zero; clean file exits zero.
- Close-out: `vc-x1 -V` reports `0.71.0`; full `cargo test` green.

## Out of scope (note in close-out, not built)
- Printing the *resolved/effective* merged config (vs. the schema of
  defaults) — a possible follow-up.
- Reworking the untyped workspace map into a typed `WorkspaceConfig`
  struct — the registry gives discoverability without that churn.

short-key row — not worth ~216 more aligned rows.

### Config-hierarchy scope (deferred)

At review the knob's resolution was raised: `[bot-session].items`
resolves CLI > workspace `.vc-config.toml` > user config >
built-in, but `--col-width` (and the existing `--result-lines`)
are plain clap defaults with no config layer. Bringing both
scalar args under that hierarchy is a config-plumbing change
(args → `Option`, new config keys, resolution in the op) folded
into Todo #12 (config discoverability) rather than done here, so
this cycle stays a single focused knob.

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)

**feat: config discoverability + scalar hierarchy.** vc-x1 has
two config homes — user `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml` (typed
`UserConfig`) and workspace `.vc-config.toml` (untyped flat map,
read key-by-key) — with no way to discover settable keys or
catch typos (unknown keys are silently ignored), and the
`bot-session` scalar knobs (`--result-lines`, `--col-width`)
have no config layer while `items` does. A single code-declared
**schema registry** becomes the source of truth that the new
`config` command, init's commented defaults, and a `--validate`
check all derive from — so they can't drift. Plan:
[toasty-coalescing-petal](../../.claude/plans/toasty-coalescing-petal.md).

   - 0.71.0-3 feat: config print command
   - 0.71.0-4 feat: init commented config defaults
   - 0.71.0-5 feat: config --validate check
   - 0.71.0 feat: config discoverability + scalar hierarchy
     (close-out)

file.content:
cycle-protocol.md Close-out ("Add an `### As-built
    ladder`…") — is in the byte-identical shared doc set
    (vc-x1, vc-template-x1, iiac-perf), so the doc edit
    needs a coordinated three-project sync, not a
    mid-cycle local change.
12. **Config discoverability: commented defaults +
    config-print command.** Settable config keys (today
    `[workspace]` path/other-repo, `[bot-session]` items) are
    only discoverable via docs; unknown keys are silently
    ignored. Live default values in a generated file would
    silently pin choices at generation time and go stale on
    upgrade, so:
    - init emits every settable key with its default value
      commented out (sshd_config style) — documents the
      surface, pins nothing.
    - a config-print subcommand (name open: `config
      --defaults`?) prints the complete annotated schema from
      the installed binary — derived from code, cannot drift;
      diff against your file to see what's new.
    - consider `validate-config`: flag unknown/misspelled
      keys in `.vc-config.toml` / user config.
    - applies to both config homes (workspace + user).
    - **bot-session scalar args honor the config hierarchy.**
      `[bot-session].items` already resolves CLI > workspace
      `.vc-config.toml` > user config > built-in, but the
      scalar knobs `--result-lines` and `--col-width` are
      plain clap defaults with no config layer. Give them
      `[bot-session].result-lines` / `[bot-session].col-width`
      keys resolving through the same four levels:
      - the args become `Option<usize>` (drop
        `default_value_t`) so "absent" is distinguishable from
        "defaulted", mirroring the `Option<bool>` item toggles.
      - resolution moves into the op (needs `ctx.user_config` +
        workspace config), not `TryFrom` (no `Context`);
        generalize `workspace_items()` to read all three keys.
      - `toml_simple` yields strings, so parse to `usize` and
        error on a bad value (consistent with malformed-file =
        fatal).
13. **bot-session: --fields / --unknown output needs
    clarification.** The inventory views work but their

input.old_string:
12. **Config discoverability: commented defaults +
    config-print command.** Settable config keys (today
    `[workspace]` path/other-repo, `[bot-session]` items) are
    only discoverable via docs; unknown keys are silently
    ignored. Live default values in a generated file would
    silently pin choices at generation time and go stale on
    upgrade, so:
    - init emits every settable key with its default value
      commented out (sshd_config style) — documents the
      surface, pins nothing.
    - a config-print subcommand (name open: `config
      --defaults`?) prints the complete annotated schema from
      the installed binary — derived from code, cannot drift;
      diff against your file to see what's new.
    - consider `validate-config`: flag unknown/misspelled
      keys in `.vc-config.toml` / user config.
    - applies to both config homes (workspace + user).
    - **bot-session scalar args honor the config hierarchy.**
      `[bot-session].items` already resolves CLI > workspace
      `.vc-config.toml` > user config > built-in, but the
      scalar knobs `--result-lines` and `--col-width` are
      plain clap defaults with no config layer. Give them
      `[bot-session].result-lines` / `[bot-session].col-width`
      keys resolving through the same four levels:
      - the args become `Option<usize>` (drop
        `default_value_t`) so "absent" is distinguishable from
        "defaulted", mirroring the `Option<bool>` item toggles.
      - resolution moves into the op (needs `ctx.user_config` +
        workspace config), not `TryFrom` (no `Context`);
        generalize `workspace_items()` to read all three keys.
      - `toml_simple` yields strings, so parse to `usize` and
        error on a bad value (consistent with malformed-file =
        fatal).
13. **bot-session: --fields / --unknown output needs

message.content: Plans should be in the TODO ladder and chores not in .claude. Also, do this change on a branch and I'll review prior to close-out. Any questions?

content.text: Two clear instructions, and they align with what I'd already started (ladder is in `## In Progress`, chores section opened; I'd removed the `.claude/plans` link). Before I continue, a few things to confirm since you asked — these change how I proceed:

questions.question: What should the work-repo branch (jj bookmark) be named?

options.label: config-discoverability
options.description: Descriptive, matches the cycle's chores/close-out title. Recommended.

options.label: todo-12-config
options.description: Ties the branch to the Todo it implements.

questions.question: How do you want to review the work commits before close-out?

options.label: Carry through, review before close-out
options.description: I commit all work steps (-0..-5) locally on the branch without per-commit check-ins, then stop for your review before the close-out. Delegated cycle. Recommended.

options.label: Per-commit description review
options.description: I stop at each work step to show the diff + commit title/body and wait for approval before committing. Full interactive protocol, slower.

questions.question: Where will you review the branch prior to close-out?

options.label: Local (jj diff/log)
options.description: Keep everything local; you review with jj / your editor. No pushes until close-out is approved. Recommended.

options.label: Push branch to remote
options.description: I push the branch to origin after the work commits so you can review it on GitHub before close-out (interim push needs your ok).

content.text:
Understood — this is a delegated unattended run. To confirm my marching orders:

- Branch `config-discoverability`; **each ladder step is its own commit**, and I'll `vc-x1 push config-discoverability` each step to the remote as I go.
- I carry through `-0`…`-5` on my own, no per-commit check-ins, implementation on cheaper models (Sonnet).
- **Hard stop before the close-out commit** — I'll push `-5`, then hand the branch to you for review; I do *not* commit close-out until you're back.
- I'll report each commit/push as it lands so you can catch up.

Let me first finish prep bookkeeping (renumber the Todo list after removing #12), then de-risk the branch-push mechanics before committing anything.

content.subject: 0.71.0-0 Preparation: version bump + cycle setup
content.description: Bump Cargo.toml/lock to 0.71.0-0; move Todo #12 to In Progress with the ladder; open chores section in chores-13.md with empty Commits:; backfill the 0.70.3 --col-width Commits: (now permanent on main).

content.subject: 0.71.0-1 bot-session scalar config keys
content.description: Make --result-lines/--col-width honor CLI>workspace>user>builtin. Args to Option<usize>; add bot-session.result-lines/col-width to UserConfig+load_from; generalize workspace_items()->workspace_bot_session(); resolve in the op mirroring resolve_items. Tests + README. (Sonnet)

content.subject: 0.71.0-2 config schema registry
content.description: New src/config_schema.rs: ConfigKey table (path, homes, kind, default, required, dynamic, doc) + schema() fn enumerating every settable key across both homes. Unit tests: unique paths, defaults match source consts. (Sonnet)

content.subject: 0.71.0-3 config command: print annotated schema
content.description: New src/config_cmd.rs following validate_desc template; wire into main.rs. `vc-x1 config` prints schema sshd_config-style; --home user|workspace|all filters. tests/cli_config.rs. (Sonnet)

content.subject: 0.71.0-4 init emits commented defaults
content.description: src/init.rs: render_vc_config(role) generating required active [workspace] block + commented optional keys from the registry. Update init tests/fixtures that byte-compare old constants. (Sonnet)

content.subject: 0.71.0-5 config --validate unknown-key check
content.description: src/config_cmd.rs --validate mode: load user + workspace config files, flag keys not in schema (honor dynamic patterns), exit non-zero on unknowns. Tests. (Sonnet)

content.content: 552:pub fn verify_tracking(repo: &Path, bookmark: &str) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {

toolUseResult.stdout: 552:pub fn verify_tracking(repo: &Path, bookmark: &str) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {

content.content:
552	pub fn verify_tracking(repo: &Path, bookmark: &str) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
553	    let repo_str = repo.to_string_lossy();
554	    let cwd = Path::new(".");
555	    let all = run(
556	        "jj",
557	        &["bookmark", "list", "-a", bookmark, "-R", &repo_str],
558	        cwd,
559	    )?;
560	    if let Some(remote) = find_non_tracking_remote(&all, bookmark) {
561	        return Err(format!(
562	            "bookmark '{bookmark}' has non-tracking remote '{bookmark}@{remote}' — \
563	             run `jj bookmark track {bookmark} --remote={remote} -R {repo_str}` to fix"
564	        )
565	        .into());
566	    }
567	    Ok(())
568	}
569	
570	/// At-rest publish state of a bookmark vs its `@origin` counterpart.
571	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
572	pub enum PublishState {
573	    /// Local bookmark and `bookmark@origin` point at the same commit.
574	    InSync,
575	    /// `bookmark@origin` does not exist — never pushed.
576	    NeverPushed,
577	    /// Local and origin point at different commits (full commit ids).
578	    Mismatch { local: String, remote: String },
579	}
580	
581	/// Resolve the at-rest publish state of `bookmark` in `repo`.
582	///
583	/// The bookmark only moves inside a push / squash-push run, which
584	/// publishes it in the same invocation — so between commands
585	/// `bookmark == bookmark@origin` is the invariant. A mismatch means
586	/// an earlier publish was lost (or origin moved out from under us).
587	///
588	/// - Read-only: two `jj log` commit-id lookups, nothing else.
589	/// - Errors when the local bookmark itself does not resolve.
590	pub fn bookmark_publish_state(
591	    repo: &Path,
592	    bookmark: &str,
593	) -> Result<PublishState, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
594	    let repo_str = repo.to_string_lossy();
595	    let cwd = Path::new(".");
596	    let commit_id = |rev: &str| -> Result<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
597	        Ok(run(
598	            "jj",
599	            &[
600	                "log",
601	                "-r",
602	                rev,
603	                "--no-graph",
604	                "-T",
605	                "commit_id",
606	                "-R",
607	                &repo_str,
608	            ],
609	            cwd,
610	        )?
611	        .trim()

input.prompt:
You are implementing one commit in a Rust project at /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1. Make the code changes and validate them. DO NOT commit, DO NOT push, DO NOT touch version numbers or notes files — only the source/test/README changes described. Report back what you changed.

# Goal
Make the `bot-session` scalar knobs `--result-lines` and `--col-width` honor the config-override hierarchy CLI > workspace `.vc-config.toml` > user config > built-in, exactly like `[bot-session].items` already does. Today they are plain clap defaults with no config layer.

# Background (already true in the code)
- `src/bot_session.rs`: `--result-lines` default is `RESULT_LINE_CAP` (=10, const near top); `--col-width` default is `COL_WIDTH` (=68). Both args currently use `default_value_t = <CONST>` and are plain `usize`.
- `BotSessionParams` (around line 346-357) stores `result_lines: usize` and `col_width: usize`.
- `TryFrom<&BotSessionArgs> for BotSessionParams` copies them straight over.
- `bot_session(ctx, params)` (around line 428) dispatches views: `View::Raw => raw_view(params)`, `View::Fields{unknown_only,per_line} => fields_view(params, unknown_only, per_line)`, `View::Conversation => {}` then does item resolution + `render(...)`.
- `fields_view(params, unknown_only, per_line)` (around line 525) uses `params.col_width` at the aggregated `info!("  {:<width$} ...", width = params.col_width)` site and passes `params.col_width` to `per_line_view(...)`.
- `per_line_view(..., col_width: usize)` already takes a `col_width` param (added recently).
- `render(&t, &items, params.result_lines, ...)` is called in the Conversation branch and uses `result_lines` for the tool-result cap.
- `workspace_items()` (around line 766-776) reads `bot-session.items` from the workspace `.vc-config.toml` via `crate::toml_simple::toml_load` (returns `HashMap<String,String>` — ALL VALUES ARE STRINGS). It's consumed at line ~465-468 for item resolution.
- `resolve_items(&params.toggles, config_items)` layers CLI toggles > workspace/user config > builtin. Mirror this layering for the scalars.
- `src/config.rs`: `UserConfig` struct (lines 78-100) derives Default; has `bot_session_items: Option<String>` set in `load_from` (~line 159) via `map.get("bot-session.items").cloned()`. There are UserConfig struct-literal sites in tests around lines 618 and 758 (and possibly others — grep for `bot_session_items:` to find every construction site).

# Changes

## 1. src/config.rs
- Add two fields to `UserConfig`: `bot_session_result_lines: Option<usize>` and `bot_session_col_width: Option<usize>` (with doc comments matching the style of the existing `bot_session_items` field doc).
- In `load_from`, parse the two new keys from `map`. Values are strings, so parse to `usize`; a PRESENT-but-unparseable value must be a hard error (consistent with the "malformed file → fatal" behavior). Absent key → `None`. Write a small helper, e.g.:
  ```rust
  fn parse_usize_key(map: &std::collections::HashMap<String, String>, key: &str)
      -> Result<Option<usize>, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
      match map.get(key) {
          None => Ok(None),
          Some(s) => s.parse::<usize>().map(Some).map_err(|e|
              format!("config key {key:?}: invalid usize {s:?}: {e}").into()),
      }
  }
  ```
  and set the two fields via `parse_usize_key(&map, "bot-session.result-lines")?` / `"bot-session.col-width"`.
- Update EVERY `UserConfig { ... }` struct-literal construction site (grep `bot_session_items:`) to include the two new fields (`bot_session_result_lines: None, bot_session_col_width: None` for the existing ones).
- Add a `#[cfg(test)]` unit test: a config file with `[bot-session]\nresult-lines = 3\ncol-width = 40` parses to `Some(3)`/`Some(40)`; and a bad value (`col-width = "abc"`) makes `load_from` return `Err`.

## 2. src/bot_session.rs
- Change the `--result-lines` and `--col-width` clap args to `Option<usize>` (DROP `default_value_t`). Update their doc comments to note the default value and the config key, e.g. col-width: "First-column ... width [default: 68; or [bot-session].col-width]".
- Change `BotSessionParams.result_lines` and `.col_width` to `Option<usize>` (RAW, unresolved). Update the `TryFrom` impl accordingly (just pass the `Option`s through).
- Replace `workspace_items()` with `workspace_bot_session()` returning a small struct:
  ```rust
  struct WorkspaceBotSession { items: Option<String>, result_lines: Option<usize>, col_width: Option<usize> }
  ```
  It reads the workspace `.vc-config.toml` once (same guard: no workspace / no file → all `None`) and parses `bot-session.result-lines` / `bot-session.col-width` to `usize`, erroring on a bad value (reuse the same parse-usize approach — you may duplicate a tiny local parser or factor a shared helper; keep it simple).
- In `bot_session(ctx, params)`: near the top (before the view `match`), call `workspace_bot_session()?` once and resolve both scalars:
  ```rust
  let col_width = params.col_width.or(ws.col_width).or(ctx.user_config.bot_session_col_width).unwrap_or(COL_WIDTH);
  let result_lines = params.result_lines.or(ws.result_lines).or(ctx.user_config.bot_session_result_lines).unwrap_or(RESULT_LINE_CAP);
  ```
  (Note: result_lines resolves against bot_session_result_lines, col_width against bot_session_col_width — do not cross them.)
- Pass the resolved `col_width` into `fields_view` (add a `col_width: usize` parameter; use it at the aggregated `info!` site and pass it to `per_line_view` INSTEAD of `params.col_width`).
- In the Conversation branch, use the already-resolved `ws.items` for item resolution (replace the old `workspace_items()` call) and pass the resolved `result_lines` into `render(...)` instead of `params.result_lines`.
- Make sure raw_view still works (it needs neither scalar).

## 3. README.md
- In the bot-session section, note that `--result-lines` and `--col-width` now resolve through the same CLI > workspace `.vc-config.toml` > user config > built-in hierarchy as `[bot-session].items`, with config keys `[bot-session].result-lines` and `[bot-session].col-width`. Keep it brief and match the existing prose style.

## 4. tests/cli_bot_session.rs
- Look at existing tests `cli_bot_session_workspace_items` and `cli_bot_session_config_items` for how they write a `.vc-config.toml` / user config into the fixture tempdir and run the CLI. Add tests that:
  - a workspace `.vc-config.toml` with `[bot-session]\ncol-width = 20` makes `--per-line` pad to 20 (assert `format!("  {:<20} {:<9}", "type", "str")` appears), and a CLI `--col-width 12` OVERRIDES it (pads to 12).
  - similarly a workspace `result-lines = 2` caps a tool result to 2 lines (mirror `cli_bot_session_result_lines`), overridden by `--result-lines`.
  Follow whatever mechanism those existing tests use to point the CLI at a workspace/user config (env var, cwd, or a flag).

# Validate (run all, must pass)
- `cargo fmt`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`
If clippy or tests fail, fix and re-run until green. Note: the project lints `unwrap_used`/`expect_used` as warnings (→ errors under -D warnings), so avoid `.unwrap()`/`.expect()` in non-test code; if genuinely needed, add `#[allow(clippy::unwrap_used)]` + a `// OK: <reason>` comment and mention it in your report.

Report: the exact files/functions you changed, the final resolution code, how the workspace-config tests point at the config, and confirmation that fmt/clippy/test are all green.

content.text:
Confirmed. All good — fmt, clippy, and full test suite (`cargo test`) are green, including the new config unit tests and the new CLI tests.

## Summary

**src/config.rs**
- Added `bot_session_result_lines: Option<usize>` and `bot_session_col_width: Option<usize>` fields to `UserConfig`.
- Added a `parse_usize_key` helper: absent key → `None`, present-but-unparseable → `Err` naming the key/value.
- `load_from` now parses `bot-session.result-lines` / `bot-session.col-width` via that helper.
- Updated all `UserConfig { .. }` struct-literal sites (both in `src/config.rs` tests and `src/init/tests.rs`) to include the two new fields.
- Added tests `bot_session_scalars_parse` (parses `result-lines = 3` / `col-width = 40`) and `bot_session_scalar_bad_value_errors` (`col-width = "abc"` → `Err`).

**src/bot_session.rs**
- `--result-lines` / `--col-width` clap args changed from `usize` with `default_value_t` to plain `Option<usize>`, with doc comments noting `[default: N; or [bot-session].<key>]`.
- `BotSessionParams.result_lines` / `.col_width` are now `Option<usize>` (raw/unresolved); `TryFrom` just copies the `Option`s through.
- Replaced `workspace_items()` with `workspace_bot_session() -> Result<WorkspaceBotSession, _>`, a struct with `items`, `result_lines`, `col_width`, all parsed from the workspace `.vc-config.toml` (same "no workspace/file → all `None`", "malformed → error" behavior).
- In `bot_session()`, resolution now happens once up front, before the view `match`:
  ```rust
  let ws = workspace_bot_session()?;
  let col_width = params.col_width.or(ws.col_width).or(ctx.user_config.bot_session_col_width).unwrap_or(COL_WIDTH);
  let result_lines = params.result_lines.or(ws.result_lines).or(ctx.user_config.bot_session_result_lines).unwrap_or(RESULT_LINE_CAP);
  ```
  `col_width` is passed into `fields_view(params, unknown_only, per_line, col_width)`; the Conversation branch uses `ws.items` (instead of a second workspace-file read) and passes resolved `result_lines` into `render(...)`.

# Goal

- `TryFrom<&BotSessionArgs> for BotSessionParams` copies them straight over.
- `bot_session(ctx, params)` (around line 428) dispatches views: `View::Raw => raw_view(params)`, `View::Fields{unknown_only,per_line} => fields_view(params, unknown_only, per_line)`, `View::Conversation => {}` then does item resolution + `render(...)`.

- `per_line_view(..., col_width: usize)` already takes a `col_width` param (added recently).
- `render(&t, &items, params.result_lines, ...)` is called in the Conversation branch and uses `result_lines` for the tool-result cap.
- `workspace_items()` (around line 766-776) reads `bot-session.items` from the workspace `.vc-config.toml` via `crate::toml_simple::toml_load` (returns `HashMap<String,String>` — ALL VALUES ARE STRINGS). It's consumed at line ~465-468 for item resolution.

- `src/config.rs`: `UserConfig` struct (lines 78-100) derives Default; has `bot_session_items: Option<String>` set in `load_from` (~line 159) via `map.get("bot-session.items").cloned()`. There are UserConfig struct-literal sites in tests around lines 618 and 758 (and possibly others — grep for `bot_session_items:` to find every construction site).

## 1. src/config.rs

  ```rust
  fn parse_usize_key(map: &std::collections::HashMap<String, String>, key: &str)
      -> Result<Option<usize>, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
      match map.get(key) {
          None => Ok(None),
          Some(s) => s.parse::<usize>().map(Some).map_err(|e|
  ```

15	  - [init](#init)
16	  - [symlink](#symlink)
17	  - [sync](#sync)
18	  - [revert](#revert)
19	  - [squash-push](#squash-push)
20	  - [push](#push)
21	  - [Testing push + squash-push](#testing-push--squash-push)
22	  - [Testing the ochid-trailer guard](#testing-the-ochid-trailer-guard)
23	- [Cross-repo Linking with Git Trailers](#cross-repo-linking-with-git-trailers)
24	- [jj Tips for Git Users](#jj-tips-for-git-users)
25	- [Thoughts for the future](#thoughts-for-the-future)
26	- [Contributing](#contributing)
27	- [License](#license)
28	
29	This is experiment 1 to explore creating a Vibe Coding (vc) environment.
30	We will investigate ways of using the dual jj-git repo concept, explored
31	in [hw-jjg-bot](https://github.com/winksaville/hw-jjg-bot.git) to
32	initially make it easy to see how the code base evolved. This is
33	made possible by the fact that we have two repos one with the code
34	and one with the conversation with the bot.
35	
36	I've chosen the jj-git environment because jj provides the concept that
37	each commit has an immutable changeID as well as the mutable commitID
38	of git. The idea is that each commit made on repo A writes the
39	changeID in the commit message to repo B. Thus there is a cross reference
40	between the two repos and this will allow vc-x1 to show how the repo
41	evolved and the entity (bot or human) can more clearly understand **how** and
42	most importantly **why** the code evolved.
43	
44	The solution space is wide open, from trivial CLI, web or app based
45	(mobile/non-mobile). In addition, I could see this as an extension to
46	existing programming editors like vscode and zed or even creating our
47	own IDE for vc.
48	
49	See [Initial commit with dual jj-git repos](./notes/chores/chores-01.md#initial-commit-with-dual-jj-git-repos)
50	for how the initial commit was created with the dual jj-git repos. After
51	doing so and I then created this README.md file.
52	
53	## Terminology
54	
55	A vc-x1 workspace pairs two jj-git repos, and this README names
56	their two sides **work** and **bot**:
57	
58	- **work repo** — the repo at the project root, holding the work
59	  product itself (source code, docs — the repo you would have
60	  anyway).
61	- **bot repo** — the `.claude` repo nested inside it, holding the
62	  bot's session data (the conversation that produced the work).
63	- **work** / **bot** — the two sides as roles, used wherever a
64	  command or doc needs to say which repo it means (e.g. push's
65	  `commit-work` / `commit-bot` stages).
66	
67	One historical wrinkle: the `-s`/`--scope` flag still spells the
68	work side `code` (`-s code`, `-s code,bot`) — that keyword predates
69	the work/bot naming.
70	
71	## Usage
72	
73	```
74	vc-x1 list [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # List commits in a jj repo
75	vc-x1 desc [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Show full description of a commit
76	vc-x1 chid [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Print changeID(s) for a revision
77	vc-x1 show [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Show commit details and diff summary
78	vc-x1 bot-session <FILE> [OPTS]        # Display a session transcript as a conversation
79	vc-x1 validate-desc [OPTS]                 # Validate commit descriptions
80	vc-x1 fix-desc [OPTS]                     # Fix commit descriptions (dry-run default)
81	vc-x1 validate-todo [FILE]                # Check todo-file entry numbering
82	vc-x1 fix-todo [FILE]                     # Renumber todo file (dry-run default)
83	vc-x1 validate-bot [OPTS]                 # Check the bot repo is published
84	vc-x1 clone <REPO> [NAME] [OPTS]          # Clone a dual-repo project
85	vc-x1 init <TARGET> [OPTS]                # Create a new dual-repo project
86	vc-x1 symlink [TARGET] [OPTS]             # Create Claude Code project symlink
87	vc-x1 sync [OPTS]                          # Fetch + sync both repos to their remotes
88	vc-x1 revert [OPTS]                        # Restore repos to a prior operation
89	vc-x1 squash-push [BOOKMARK] [OPTS]        # Squash @ into @-, advance a bookmark, push
90	vc-x1 push [BOOKMARK] [OPTS]               # Commit both repos, push work, squash-push bot
91	vc-x1 --version                            # Print version
92	vc-x1 --help                           # Print help
93	```
94	
95	**The `..` notation:** dots on the revision show which direction to
96	list commits:
97	
98	- `x..` — x at top, ancestors below (older commits)
99	- `..x` — descendants above (newer commits), x at bottom
100	- `..x..` — both directions, x in the middle
101	
102	COMMITS is the total number of commits to show (x is always included).
103	Without dots, `x` with a count defaults to `x..` (ancestors).
104	For `..x..`, the budget is split: ancestors get the extra on odd counts.
105	
106	```
107	vc-x1 list -r @              # just @ (1 commit)
108	vc-x1 list -r @.. -n 5       # @ + 4 ancestors (5 commits, @ at top)
109	vc-x1 list -r ..@ -n 3       # 2 descendants + @ (3 commits, @ at bottom)
110	vc-x1 list -r ..@.. -n 3     # 1 descendant, @, 1 ancestor (3 commits)
111	vc-x1 list -r ..@.. -n 4     # 1 descendant, @, 2 ancestors (4 commits)
112	```
113	
114	### Shell completion
115	
116	vc-x1 provides tab completion for all subcommands and flags using claps
117	[unstable-dynamic](https://docs.rs/clap_complete/latest/clap_complete/env/struct.CompleteEnv.html)
118	only feature. It is a "simple" implementation and does not handle completing
119	revisions but still useful. To enable it, add one of the following to your shell's startup file:
120	
121	```bash
122	# bash (~/.bashrc)
123	source <(COMPLETE=bash vc-x1)
124	
125	# zsh (~/.zshrc)
126	source <(COMPLETE=zsh vc-x1)
127	
128	# fish (~/.config/fish/config.fish)
129	source (COMPLETE=fish vc-x1 | psub)
130	```
131	
132	Completions are generated dynamically by the binary, so they stay in
133	sync with the installed version automatically.
134	
135	### Positional shorthand
136	
137	REVISION and COMMITS can be given as positional arguments, omitting
138	`-r` and `-n`:
139	
140	```
141	vc-x1 list x                # just x (1 commit)
142	vc-x1 list x 5              # x.. 5 → x + 4 ancestors (5 commits)
143	vc-x1 list x.. 5            # same as above
144	vc-x1 list ..x 3            # 2 descendants + x (3 commits)
145	vc-x1 list ..x.. 3          # 1 descendant, x, 1 ancestor (3 commits)
146	vc-x1 list ..x.. 1          # just x (1 commit)
147	```
148	
149	**Defaults:** with no arguments, REVISION is `@` and COMMITS is 1
150	(just the working copy).
151	
152	Named flags `-r`/`--revision`, `-n`/`--commits`, `-R`/`--repo`, and
153	`-s`/`--scope` take precedence over positional arguments.
154	
155	### Multi-repo queries
156	
157	`-s`/`--scope` selects workspace sides by role keyword; `code` is the
158	work repo, `bot` is the `.claude` repo, `code,bot` is both. The
159	workspace root is found by walking up from cwd (the existing
160	`find_workspace_root` rule).
161	
162	```
163	vc-x1 chid -s code,bot          # both sides of the workspace
164	vc-x1 chid -s code              # just the work repo
165	vc-x1 chid -s bot               # just the bot repo
166	vc-x1 list @.. 3 -s code,bot    # works with chid / desc / list / show
167	```
168	
169	`-R`/`--repo` takes a single path. Used alone it points at one jj
170	repo (workspace lookup is skipped); combined with `-s` it overrides
171	the workspace root for the role lookup:
172	
173	```
174	vc-x1 chid -R .                  # single repo at .
175	vc-x1 chid -R .claude            # single repo at .claude
176	vc-x1 chid -R ../other -s code,bot   # both sides of ../other workspace
177	```
178	
179	Defaults preserve prior behavior — no flag → `[.]`, `-R foo` alone
180	→ `[foo]`. When multiple repos are resolved (any `-s` that names
181	more than one role), output is labeled with bold `=== path ===`

content.subject: 0.71.0-0 Preparation: version bump + cycle setup
content.description: Bump Cargo.toml/lock to 0.71.0-0; move Todo #12 to In Progress with the ladder; open chores section in chores-13.md with empty Commits:; backfill the 0.70.3 --col-width Commits: (now permanent on main).

content.subject: 0.71.0-1 bot-session scalar config keys
content.description: Make --result-lines/--col-width honor CLI>workspace>user>builtin. Args to Option<usize>; add bot-session.result-lines/col-width to UserConfig+load_from; generalize workspace_items()->workspace_bot_session(); resolve in the op mirroring resolve_items. Tests + README. (Sonnet)

content.subject: 0.71.0-2 config schema registry
content.description: New src/config_schema.rs: ConfigKey table (path, homes, kind, default, required, dynamic, doc) + schema() fn enumerating every settable key across both homes. Unit tests: unique paths, defaults match source consts. (Sonnet)

content.subject: 0.71.0-3 config command: print annotated schema
content.description: New src/config_cmd.rs following validate_desc template; wire into main.rs. `vc-x1 config` prints schema sshd_config-style; --home user|workspace|all filters. tests/cli_config.rs. (Sonnet)

content.subject: 0.71.0-4 init emits commented defaults
content.description: src/init.rs: render_vc_config(role) generating required active [workspace] block + commented optional keys from the registry. Update init tests/fixtures that byte-compare old constants. (Sonnet)

content.subject: 0.71.0-5 config --validate unknown-key check
content.description: src/config_cmd.rs --validate mode: load user + workspace config files, flag keys not in schema (honor dynamic patterns), exit non-zero on unknowns. Tests. (Sonnet)

content.subject: 0.71.0 Close-out
content.description: README config command + init behavior; TODO #12 -> Done with chores ref; move In Progress block into chores with As-built ladder + Single-source-of-truth design subsection; notes/README.md. Full cargo cycle; push shape decision. (Fable 5)

.vc-config.toml > user config > built-in, exactly like
[bot-session].items already does. Previously both were plain
clap defaults with no config layer.

  - add bot_session_result_lines / bot_session_col_width to

- src/bot_session.rs
  - --result-lines / --col-width args → Option<usize> (drop
    default_value_t); help notes the default + config key

    { items, result_lines, col_width }, one .vc-config.toml read

  struct literal

input.prompt:
You are implementing one commit in a Rust project at /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1. Create a new module and wire it in. DO NOT commit, DO NOT push, DO NOT change version numbers or notes files. Report what you changed.

# Goal
Add a single code-declared **config schema registry**: the source of truth enumerating every settable config key across vc-x1's two config homes. Later commits (a `config` print command, init's commented defaults, a `--validate` check) will all derive from this so they cannot drift. THIS commit adds only the registry + tests; no user-visible behavior yet.

# Deliverable: new file src/config_schema.rs
Module docstring (`//!`) explaining it's the single source of truth for settable config keys. Follow the project's doc-comment convention (every item gets a `///` doc comment; prose form = short intro + bullets).

Define:
```rust
/// Which config home a key belongs to.
/// - `User` — ~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml
/// - `WorkspaceCode` / `WorkspaceBot` — <root>/.vc-config.toml in
///   the work repo vs the .claude bot repo
pub enum Home { User, WorkspaceCode, WorkspaceBot }

/// The value shape a key expects (for rendering / future typed use).
pub enum ValueKind { Str, Usize, ItemList }

/// One settable configuration key.
pub struct ConfigKey {
    pub path: &'static str,          // dotted TOML path, e.g. "bot-session.col-width"
    pub homes: &'static [Home],      // which home(s) accept it
    pub kind: ValueKind,
    pub default: Option<&'static str>, // rendered default, None if no default
    pub required: bool,              // active (not commented) in init; role-specific value
    pub dynamic: bool,               // path has a <placeholder> segment (matches a family of keys)
    pub doc: &'static str,           // one-line description
}

/// The complete registry of settable keys.
pub fn schema() -> &'static [ConfigKey];
```
Derive `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq` on the enums as appropriate. `schema()` returns a `&'static [ConfigKey]` backed by a `const` or `static` array.

# The keys to enumerate (EXACT — this is the whole surface)

User home (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`):
| path | homes | kind | default | required | dynamic | doc |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `default.account` | [User] | Str | None | false | false | Default account when --account is absent |
| `default.debug` | [User] | Str | None | false | false | Default --debug value when used without an argument (reserved; not yet consumed) |
| `repo.default` | [User] | Str | None | false | false | Top-level shorthand: repo category to use when --repo is absent |
| `repo.category.<cat>` | [User] | Str | None | false | true | Top-level shorthand: literal value for repo category <cat> |
| `account.<name>.repo.default` | [User] | Str | None | false | true | Per-account default repo category |
| `account.<name>.repo.category.<cat>` | [User] | Str | None | false | true | Per-account literal value for repo category <cat> |

bot-session keys — accepted in ALL THREE homes ([User, WorkspaceCode, WorkspaceBot]):
| path | kind | default | required | dynamic | doc |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `bot-session.items` | ItemList | Some("headers,user,assistant,tool,summary") | false | false | Default bot-session item set (comma-separated) |
| `bot-session.result-lines` | Usize | Some("10") | false | false | Default --result-lines: max lines shown per tool result (0 = unlimited) |
| `bot-session.col-width` | Usize | Some("68") | false | false | Default --col-width: first-column width in the field-inventory views |

Workspace home only ([WorkspaceCode, WorkspaceBot]):
| path | kind | default | required | dynamic | doc |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `workspace.path` | Str | None | true | false | This repo's path relative to the workspace root (role-specific: "/" for the work repo, "/.claude" for the bot repo) |
| `workspace.other-repo` | Str | None | false | false | Relative path to the counterpart repo; presence signals dual-repo mode (role-specific: ".claude" for the work repo, ".." for the bot repo) |
| `push.state-dir` | Str | Some(".vc-x1") | false | false | Directory (relative to repo root) holding the push state file |
| `push.state-file` | Str | Some("push-state.toml") | false | false | Filename of the push state file under push.state-dir |

Notes:
- `workspace.path` / `workspace.other-repo` have role-specific values, so their `default` is `None` here (the doc records the per-role values; init will emit the concrete values by role in a later commit). `workspace.path` is `required: true`.
- The three `bot-session.*` keys appear ONCE each with `homes: &[Home::User, Home::WorkspaceCode, Home::WorkspaceBot]`.

# Drift-protection unit tests (in src/config_schema.rs #[cfg(test)])
The numeric/string defaults must stay in lockstep with their source constants. To let the test reference them, change these four constants to `pub(crate)` (add `pub(crate)` — do not move or rename them):
- `src/bot_session.rs`: `const COL_WIDTH` (=68) and `const RESULT_LINE_CAP` (=10) → `pub(crate) const ...`
- `src/push.rs`: `const DEFAULT_STATE_DIR` (".vc-x1") and `const DEFAULT_STATE_FILE` ("push-state.toml") → `pub(crate) const ...`

Then tests:
- `defaults_match_source_consts`: assert the schema's `bot-session.col-width` default == `crate::bot_session::COL_WIDTH.to_string()`, `bot-session.result-lines` == `crate::bot_session::RESULT_LINE_CAP.to_string()`, `push.state-dir` == `crate::push::DEFAULT_STATE_DIR`, `push.state-file` == `crate::push::DEFAULT_STATE_FILE`. (Find each key in `schema()` by `path`.)
- `paths_unique`: no two `ConfigKey`s share a `path`.
- `homes_non_empty`: every key has at least one `Home`.
- `dynamic_keys_have_placeholder`: every key with `dynamic == true` contains a `<` in its `path`, and every key WITHOUT a `<` has `dynamic == false`.

# Wire-in
- Add `mod config_schema;` to the `mod` block at the top of `src/main.rs` (keep it in the existing alphabetical-ish order near `mod config;`).
- The module is currently unused by non-test code, which will trip `dead_code` under clippy -D warnings. Handle it cleanly: add `#[allow(dead_code)]` on the `config_schema` module items (or the `mod` declaration) with a short `// OK:`-style note that the consumers land in the following commits. Prefer allowing at the module or item level over leaving warnings.

# Validate (must all pass)
- `cargo fmt`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`
Fix and re-run until green. Avoid `.unwrap()`/`.expect()` in non-test code (project lints them); tests may use them.

Report: the final `ConfigKey` list you encoded (paths only is fine), the exact enum/struct definitions, which consts you made pub(crate), how you handled the dead_code lint, and confirmation fmt/clippy/test are green.

content.text:
All green: fmt, clippy (`-D warnings`), and the full test suite pass, including the 4 new drift-protection tests.

Summary of changes:

**New file `/home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/src/config_schema.rs`**
- `//!` module docstring stating it's the source of truth for settable config keys, and that non-test consumers land in later commits.
- `pub enum Home { User, WorkspaceCode, WorkspaceBot }` — `derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)`
- `pub enum ValueKind { Str, Usize, ItemList }` — same derives
- `pub struct ConfigKey { path, homes, kind, default, required, dynamic, doc }` (fields exactly as specified)
- `pub fn schema() -> &'static [ConfigKey]` backed by a `const SCHEMA: &[ConfigKey]` array, plus two small `const` home-slices (`BOT_SESSION_HOMES`, `WORKSPACE_HOMES`) for readability/reuse.
- 13 `ConfigKey` entries, exactly matching the spec:
  - `default.account`, `default.debug`, `repo.default`, `repo.category.<cat>`, `account.<name>.repo.default`, `account.<name>.repo.category.<cat>` (User home)
  - `bot-session.items`, `bot-session.result-lines`, `bot-session.col-width` (all three homes)
  - `workspace.path`, `workspace.other-repo`, `push.state-dir`, `push.state-file` (WorkspaceCode + WorkspaceBot)
- `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` with the four required tests: `defaults_match_source_consts`, `paths_unique`, `homes_non_empty`, `dynamic_keys_have_placeholder`.

**Consts changed to `pub(crate)`** (values/locations unchanged):
- `src/bot_session.rs`: `COL_WIDTH` (68), `RESULT_LINE_CAP` (10)
- `src/push.rs`: `DEFAULT_STATE_DIR` (".vc-x1"), `DEFAULT_STATE_FILE` ("push-state.toml")

**dead_code handling:** added `#![allow(dead_code)]` at the top of `config_schema.rs` with an `// OK: consumers land in the following commits (config print, init, --validate)` comment, rather than annotating each item individually.

**Wire-in:** added `mod config_schema;` in `src/main.rs` right after `mod config;`.

No commits made, no version bump, no notes files touched — as instructed.

| `default.account` | [User] | Str | None | false | false | Default account when --account is absent |
| `default.debug` | [User] | Str | None | false | false | Default --debug value when used without an argument (reserved; not yet consumed) |
| `repo.default` | [User] | Str | None | false | false | Top-level shorthand: repo category to use when --repo is absent |
| `repo.category.<cat>` | [User] | Str | None | false | true | Top-level shorthand: literal value for repo category <cat> |
| `account.<name>.repo.default` | [User] | Str | None | false | true | Per-account default repo category |
| `account.<name>.repo.category.<cat>` | [User] | Str | None | false | true | Per-account literal value for repo category <cat> |

bot-session keys — accepted in ALL THREE homes ([User, WorkspaceCode, WorkspaceBot]):
| path | kind | default | required | dynamic | doc |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `bot-session.items` | ItemList | Some("headers,user,assistant,tool,summary") | false | false | Default bot-session item set (comma-separated) |
| `bot-session.result-lines` | Usize | Some("10") | false | false | Default --result-lines: max lines shown per tool result (0 = unlimited) |
| `bot-session.col-width` | Usize | Some("68") | false | false | Default --col-width: first-column width in the field-inventory views |

Workspace home only ([WorkspaceCode, WorkspaceBot]):
| path | kind | default | required | dynamic | doc |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `workspace.path` | Str | None | true | false | This repo's path relative to the workspace root (role-specific: "/" for the work repo, "/.claude" for the bot repo) |
| `workspace.other-repo` | Str | None | false | false | Relative path to the counterpart repo; presence signals dual-repo mode (role-specific: ".claude" for the work repo, ".." for the bot repo) |
| `push.state-dir` | Str | Some(".vc-x1") | false | false | Directory (relative to repo root) holding the push state file |
| `push.state-file` | Str | Some("push-state.toml") | false | false | Filename of the push state file under push.state-dir |

Notes:
- `workspace.path` / `workspace.other-repo` have role-specific values, so their `default` is `None` here (the doc records the per-role values; init will emit the concrete values by role in a later commit). `workspace.path` is `required: true`.
- The three `bot-session.*` keys appear ONCE each with `homes: &[Home::User, Home::WorkspaceCode, Home::WorkspaceBot]`.

# Drift-protection unit tests (in src/config_schema.rs #[cfg(test)])
The numeric/string defaults must stay in lockstep with their source constants. To let the test reference them, change these four constants to `pub(crate)` (add `pub(crate)` — do not move or rename them):
- `src/bot_session.rs`: `const COL_WIDTH` (=68) and `const RESULT_LINE_CAP` (=10) → `pub(crate) const ...`
- `src/push.rs`: `const DEFAULT_STATE_DIR` (".vc-x1") and `const DEFAULT_STATE_FILE` ("push-state.toml") → `pub(crate) const ...`

Then tests:
- `defaults_match_source_consts`: assert the schema's `bot-session.col-width` default == `crate::bot_session::COL_WIDTH.to_string()`, `bot-session.result-lines` == `crate::bot_session::RESULT_LINE_CAP.to_string()`, `push.state-dir` == `crate::push::DEFAULT_STATE_DIR`, `push.state-file` == `crate::push::DEFAULT_STATE_FILE`. (Find each key in `schema()` by `path`.)
- `paths_unique`: no two `ConfigKey`s share a `path`.
- `homes_non_empty`: every key has at least one `Home`.
- `dynamic_keys_have_placeholder`: every key with `dynamic == true` contains a `<` in its `path`, and every key WITHOUT a `<` has `dynamic == false`.

# Wire-in
- Add `mod config_schema;` to the `mod` block at the top of `src/main.rs` (keep it in the existing alphabetical-ish order near `mod config;`).
- The module is currently unused by non-test code, which will trip `dead_code` under clippy -D warnings. Handle it cleanly: add `#[allow(dead_code)]` on the `config_schema` module items (or the `mod` declaration) with a short `// OK:`-style note that the consumers land in the following commits. Prefer allowing at the module or item level over leaving warnings.

# Validate (must all pass)
- `cargo fmt`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`
Fix and re-run until green. Avoid `.unwrap()`/`.expect()` in non-test code (project lints them); tests may use them.

Report: the final `ConfigKey` list you encoded (paths only is fine), the exact enum/struct definitions, which consts you made pub(crate), how you handled the dead_code lint, and confirmation fmt/clippy/test are green.

content.content:
1	//! Single source of truth for every settable config key across
2	//! vc-x1's two config homes (the user config file and the
3	//! per-repo workspace config).
4	//!
5	//! - This module only declares the registry — nothing in
6	//!   non-test code consumes it yet.
7	//! - Later commits derive a `config` print command, init's
8	//!   commented defaults, and a `--validate` unknown-key check
9	//!   from `schema()`, so those surfaces cannot drift from this
10	//!   list.
11	#![allow(dead_code)] // OK: consumers land in the following commits (config print, init, --validate)
12	
13	/// Which config home a key belongs to.
14	/// - `User` — `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`
15	/// - `WorkspaceCode` / `WorkspaceBot` — `<root>/.vc-config.toml`
16	///   in the work repo vs the `.claude` bot repo
17	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
18	pub enum Home {
19	    User,
20	    WorkspaceCode,
21	    WorkspaceBot,
22	}
23	
24	/// The value shape a key expects (for rendering / future typed
25	/// use).
26	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
27	pub enum ValueKind {
28	    Str,
29	    Usize,
30	    ItemList,
31	}
32	
33	/// One settable configuration key.
34	pub struct ConfigKey {
35	    /// Dotted TOML path, e.g. `"bot-session.col-width"`.
36	    pub path: &'static str,
37	    /// Which home(s) accept this key.
38	    pub homes: &'static [Home],
39	    /// The value shape this key expects.
40	    pub kind: ValueKind,
41	    /// Rendered default, `None` if there is no default.
42	    pub default: Option<&'static str>,
43	    /// Active (not commented) in init; the value is role-specific.
44	    pub required: bool,
45	    /// The path has a `<placeholder>` segment matching a family
46	    /// of keys (e.g. `repo.category.<cat>`).
47	    pub dynamic: bool,
48	    /// One-line description.
49	    pub doc: &'static str,
50	}
51	
52	/// Homes accepted by every `bot-session.*` key: all three homes,
53	/// since the user config and both workspace configs may each
54	/// carry a default.
55	const BOT_SESSION_HOMES: &[Home] = &[Home::User, Home::WorkspaceCode, Home::WorkspaceBot];
56	
57	/// Homes accepted by workspace-only keys (`workspace.*`,
58	/// `push.*`): the two `.vc-config.toml` homes, never the user
59	/// config.
60	const WORKSPACE_HOMES: &[Home] = &[Home::WorkspaceCode, Home::WorkspaceBot];
61	
62	/// The complete registry of settable keys.
63	///
64	/// - User home: `default.*`, `repo.*`, and the per-account
65	///   `account.<name>.repo.*` family.
66	/// - `bot-session.*`: accepted in all three homes.
67	/// - Workspace-only: `workspace.*`, `push.*`.
68	const SCHEMA: &[ConfigKey] = &[
69	    ConfigKey {
70	        path: "default.account",
71	        homes: &[Home::User],
72	        kind: ValueKind::Str,
73	        default: None,
74	        required: false,
75	        dynamic: false,
76	        doc: "Default account when --account is absent",
77	    },
78	    ConfigKey {
79	        path: "default.debug",
80	        homes: &[Home::User],
81	        kind: ValueKind::Str,
82	        default: None,
83	        required: false,
84	        dynamic: false,
85	        doc: "Default --debug value when used without an argument (reserved; not yet consumed)",
86	    },
87	    ConfigKey {
88	        path: "repo.default",
89	        homes: &[Home::User],
90	        kind: ValueKind::Str,
91	        default: None,
92	        required: false,
93	        dynamic: false,
94	        doc: "Top-level shorthand: repo category to use when --repo is absent",
95	    },
96	    ConfigKey {
97	        path: "repo.category.<cat>",
98	        homes: &[Home::User],
99	        kind: ValueKind::Str,
100	        default: None,
101	        required: false,
102	        dynamic: true,
103	        doc: "Top-level shorthand: literal value for repo category <cat>",
104	    },
105	    ConfigKey {
106	        path: "account.<name>.repo.default",
107	        homes: &[Home::User],
108	        kind: ValueKind::Str,
109	        default: None,
110	        required: false,
111	        dynamic: true,
112	        doc: "Per-account default repo category",
113	    },
114	    ConfigKey {
115	        path: "account.<name>.repo.category.<cat>",
116	        homes: &[Home::User],
117	        kind: ValueKind::Str,
118	        default: None,
119	        required: false,
120	        dynamic: true,
121	        doc: "Per-account literal value for repo category <cat>",
122	    },
123	    ConfigKey {
124	        path: "bot-session.items",
125	        homes: BOT_SESSION_HOMES,
126	        kind: ValueKind::ItemList,
127	        default: Some("headers,user,assistant,tool,summary"),
128	        required: false,
129	        dynamic: false,
130	        doc: "Default bot-session item set (comma-separated)",
131	    },
132	    ConfigKey {
133	        path: "bot-session.result-lines",
134	        homes: BOT_SESSION_HOMES,
135	        kind: ValueKind::Usize,
136	        default: Some("10"),
137	        required: false,
138	        dynamic: false,
139	        doc: "Default --result-lines: max lines shown per tool result (0 = unlimited)",
140	    },
141	    ConfigKey {
142	        path: "bot-session.col-width",
143	        homes: BOT_SESSION_HOMES,
144	        kind: ValueKind::Usize,
145	        default: Some("68"),
146	        required: false,
147	        dynamic: false,
148	        doc: "Default --col-width: first-column width in the field-inventory views",
149	    },
150	    ConfigKey {
151	        path: "workspace.path",
152	        homes: WORKSPACE_HOMES,
153	        kind: ValueKind::Str,
154	        default: None,
155	        required: true,
156	        dynamic: false,
157	        doc: "This repo's path relative to the workspace root (role-specific: \"/\" for the work repo, \"/.claude\" for the bot repo)",
158	    },
159	    ConfigKey {
160	        path: "workspace.other-repo",
161	        homes: WORKSPACE_HOMES,
162	        kind: ValueKind::Str,
163	        default: None,
164	        required: false,
165	        dynamic: false,
166	        doc: "Relative path to the counterpart repo; presence signals dual-repo mode (role-specific: \".claude\" for the work repo, \"..\" for the bot repo)",
167	    },
168	    ConfigKey {
169	        path: "push.state-dir",
170	        homes: WORKSPACE_HOMES,
171	        kind: ValueKind::Str,
172	        default: Some(".vc-x1"),
173	        required: false,
174	        dynamic: false,
175	        doc: "Directory (relative to repo root) holding the push state file",
176	    },
177	    ConfigKey {
178	        path: "push.state-file",
179	        homes: WORKSPACE_HOMES,
180	        kind: ValueKind::Str,
181	        default: Some("push-state.toml"),
182	        required: false,
183	        dynamic: false,
184	        doc: "Filename of the push state file under push.state-dir",
185	    },
186	];
187	
188	/// Returns the complete registry of settable config keys.
189	pub fn schema() -> &'static [ConfigKey] {
190	    SCHEMA
191	}
192	
193	#[cfg(test)]
194	mod tests {
195	    use super::*;
196	
197	    /// Finds a schema entry by its dotted path.
198	    ///
199	    /// - Panics if not found: tests only, and a missing key is a
200	    ///   test bug worth failing loudly on.
201	    #[allow(clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::expect_used)]
202	    fn find(path: &str) -> &'static ConfigKey {
203	        schema()
204	            .iter()
205	            .find(|k| k.path == path)
206	            .unwrap_or_else(|| panic!("schema key {path:?} not found"))
207	    }
208	
209	    #[test]
210	    fn defaults_match_source_consts() {
211	        assert_eq!(
212	            find("bot-session.col-width").default,
213	            Some(crate::bot_session::COL_WIDTH.to_string()).as_deref()
214	        );
215	        assert_eq!(
216	            find("bot-session.result-lines").default,
217	            Some(crate::bot_session::RESULT_LINE_CAP.to_string()).as_deref()
218	        );
219	        assert_eq!(
220	            find("push.state-dir").default,
221	            Some(crate::push::DEFAULT_STATE_DIR)
222	        );
223	        assert_eq!(
224	            find("push.state-file").default,
225	            Some(crate::push::DEFAULT_STATE_FILE)
226	        );
227	    }
228	
229	    #[test]
230	    fn paths_unique() {
231	        let mut paths: Vec<&str> = schema().iter().map(|k| k.path).collect();
232	        paths.sort_unstable();
233	        let mut deduped = paths.clone();
234	        deduped.dedup();
235	        assert_eq!(paths.len(), deduped.len(), "duplicate path(s) in schema()");
236	    }
237	
238	    #[test]
239	    fn homes_non_empty() {
240	        for key in schema() {
241	            assert!(!key.homes.is_empty(), "{} has no homes", key.path);
242	        }
243	    }
244	
245	    #[test]
246	    fn dynamic_keys_have_placeholder() {
247	        for key in schema() {
248	            let has_placeholder = key.path.contains('<');
249	            assert_eq!(
250	                key.dynamic, has_placeholder,
251	                "{}: dynamic={} but placeholder-presence={}",
252	                key.path, key.dynamic, has_placeholder
253	            );
254	        }
255	    }
256	}
257
